PARTIES AGREED TO TERMS REGARDING DISCOVERY WHICH WAS PRESENTED TO THE COURT AND STATED ON THE RECORD BY PLAINTIFF. TRIAL DATE IS SET ON 11/30/09 AT 11:15 AM IN DEPT. 206. THE COURT ORDERED ALL MOTIONS ON SHORTENED TIME, EXCEPT MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATIONS, TO BE HEARD IN DEPT. 206 WITH 8 CALENDAR DAYS NOTICE, OPPOSITION DUE 2 DAYS BEFORE, NO REPLY. MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ADJUDICATION ON 15 DAYS NOTICE AND TO BE HEARD UP TO AND INCLUDING THE DAY OF TRIAL. COURTESY COPIES TO BE EMAILED TO DEPT. 206'S RESEARCH ATTORNEY. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL IDENTIFICATION AND LAY WITNESSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS THAT SUPPORT THEIR TESTIMONY, AS TO EACH INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT BY 10/27/09. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES AFTER THE DUE DATE THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT IDENTIFIED PREVIOUSLY. ALL DEFENDANTS SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL LAY WITNESSES THAT THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT LEARNED OF THROUGH ITS OWN DISCOVERY EFFORTS AND THAT WILL SUPPORT PROPOSITION 51 PURPOSES OR FOR THEIR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS UPON WHICH THESE WITNESSES SUPPORT, AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 11/10/09. IF DEFENDANTS LEARN OF NEW WITNESSES AND OR DOCUMENTS, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED PREVIOUSLY. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS AMENDED: DEFENDANT HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S MOTION FOR ORDER REQUIRING THE PRODUCTION OF LUNG TISSUE FOR TISSUE DIGESTION AND FIBER BURDEN ANALYSIS AND PERMITTING DIVISION OF THE TISSUE AND PERFORMANCE OF PROTECTED TISSUE DIGESTION AND FIBER BURDEN ANALYSIS IS GRANTED CONDITIONED ON 1) 50% OF EACH SAMPLE PROVIDED TO A PERSON DESIGNATED BY PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL WITHIN 7 BUSINESS DAYS AFTER EACH CUT IS MADE; 2) THE TESTING BE COMPLETED WITHIN 30 DAYS AFTER DEFENDANT'S RECEIPT OF THE PATHOLOGY MATERIAL; AND 3) TISSUE REMOVED SHALL NOT BE USED FOR ANY OTHER PURPOSE OTHER THAN THAT WHICH HAS BEEN DISCLOSED. THE RESULTS OF SUCH ANALYSIS ARE PROTECTED PURSUANT TO THE WORK-PRODUCT DOCTRINE UNLESS AND UNTIL DEFENDANT WISHES TO USE THE RESULTS AT TRIAL. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: TERI L. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-25-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT TEXTRON INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
Appearances: Monica D. Williams of Tucker Ellis LLP (617.2210) for defendant and moving party; Steven M. Harowitz of Harowitz & Tigerman LLP (788.1588) for plaintiffs and responding parties.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 through 5 (negligence, strict liability, products liability, survival and loss of consortium) and granted as to issue 6 (punitive damages). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Stuckart was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The deposition testimony of Mr. Mangerich and Mr. Fisher preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Order signed. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Rhonda R. Harris, CSR #7414 (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jul-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ARVINMERITOR INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFFS' CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES.
Appearances: Stephen Tigerman of Harowitz & Tigerman, LLP (788.1588) for plaintiff and responding party; Michelle C. Jackson of McKenna Long & Aldridge LLP (267.4132) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court ruled as follows: denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production. Plaintiffs presented evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Collateral estoppel does not apply. Plaintiffs failed to demonstrate that the issues decided in Bankhead v. ArvinMeritor, Inc., (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 68, are identical with the ones presented here. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Rhonda Harris, CSR #7414 (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-12-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
David A. Pereda of NIXON PEABODY (415-984-8260), appearing for defendant / moving party Ford Motor Company
Steven M. Harowitz of HAROWITZ & TIGERMAN, LLP (415-788-1588) appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Kevin Stuckart, et al.
Stephen Tigerman of HAROWITZ & TIGERMAN, LLP (415-788-1588) appearing for plaintiff Gene Lepore

RULING:
DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

*** Prior to the hearing, the Court and Counsel discussed ruling and submission regarding the Lepore case (275411). (See details of entry in the Lepore case.)
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Ford Motor Company's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issue two (third cause of action) and denied as to issues one and three (sophisticated user doctrine and claim for punitive damages). As to the sophisticated user doctrine, plaintiffs presented evidence creating a triable issue whether the sophisticated user doctrine does not apply. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to demonstrate that Michigan law applies to that claim and failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to adjudication of the third cause of action, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant required the use of asbestos-containing friction components. Plaintiffs objections to the evidence submitted with defendant's reply are sustained. The court did not consider that evidence. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Honeywell International Inc. For Order (1) Requiring The Production Of Lung Tissue For Tissue Digestion And Fiber Burden Analysis, And (2) Permitting Division Of The Tissue And Performance Of Protected Tissue Digestion And Fiber Burden Analysis
Off Calendar May-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Richard S. Chon of PERKINS COIE (415-344-7000), appearing for defendant / moving party Honeywell International Inc.
Steven M. Harowitz of HAROWITZ & TIGERMAN, LLP (415-788-1588) appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Kevin Stuckart, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC.'S MOTION OF DEFENDANT HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC. FOR ORDER (1) REQUIRING THE PRODUCTION OF LUNG TISSUE FOR TISSUE DIGESTION AND FIBER BURDEN ANALYSIS, AND (2) PERMITTING DIVISION OF THE TISSUE AND PERFORMANCE OF PROTECTED TISSUE DIGESTION AND FIBER BURDEN ANALYSIS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Defendant Honeywell International, Inc.'s motion for order requiring the production of lung tissue for tissue digestion and fiber burden analysis and permitting division of the tissue and performance of protected tissue digestion and fiber burden analysis is granted conditioned on 1) 50% of each sample provided to a person designated by plaintiffs' counsel within 7 business days after each cut is made; 2) the testing be completed within 30 days after defendant's receipt of the pathology material; and 3) tissue removed shall not be used for any other purpose other than that which has been disclosed. The results of such analysis are protected pursuant to the work-product doctrine unless and until defendant wishes to use the results at trial. Prevailing party to prepare an order. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153 (503/TLJ)
